conversation.speaker: Rina

question.jepang: 田中(たなか)さん、来月(らいげつ)から営業部(えいぎょうぶ)に異動(いどう)することになったって聞(き)きましたけど、本当(ほんとう)ですか？
question.romaji: Tanaka-san, raigetsu kara eigyoubu ni idou suru koto ni natta tte kikimashita kedo, hontou desu ka?
question.arti: Tanaka, I heard you're being transferred to the sales department starting next month. Is that true?

choices.jepang: はい、自分(じぶん)で決(き)めたんです。
choices.romaji: Hai, jibun de kimetan desu.
choices.arti: Yes, I decided myself.

choices.jepang: いいえ、来年(らいねん)からですよ。
choices.romaji: Iie, rainen kara desu yo.
choices.arti: No, it's from next year.

choices.jepang: ええ、急(きゅう)に決(き)まったんです。正直(しょうじき)、驚(おどろ)いています。
choices.romaji: Ee, kyuu ni kimattan desu. Shoujiki, odoroite imasu.
choices.arti: Yes, it was suddenly decided. Honestly, I'm surprised.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'Yes, it was suddenly decided. Honestly, I'm surprised' because it uses ~ことになる showing someone else's decision. Meanwhile, 'I decided myself' wrongly uses ~ことにする (personal decision), and 'No, from next year' contradicts the known facts.

conversation.speaker: Rina

question.jepang: そうなんですか。営業(えいぎょう)の経験(けいけん)はあるんですか？
question.romaji: Sou nan desu ka. Eigyou no keiken wa arun desu ka?

choices.jepang: 営業(えいぎょう)は楽(たの)しいらしいですから、心配(しんぱい)していません。
choices.romaji: Eigyou wa tanoshii rashii desu kara, shinpai shite imasen.
choices.arti: I heard sales is fun, so I'm not worried.

choices.romaji: Mattaku nain desu. Dakara, idou suru mae ni sukoshi demo benkyou shite okou to omotte.
choices.arti: None at all. So before transferring, I want to study a bit.

choices.romaji: Daigaku no toki ni yatte imashita kara daijoubu desu.
choices.arti: I had experience in college, so no problem.

conversation.speaker: Rina

question.jepang: 偉(えら)いですね。何(なに)か手伝(てつだ)えることがあったら言(い)ってくださいね。

choices.romaji: Jaa, kawari ni itte kuremasen ka?
choices.arti: Then, would you replace me?

choices.romaji: Arigatou gozaimasu. Jitsu wa, eigyoubu no Yamada-san ni shigoto no yarikata wo oshiete moraou to omotte irun desu.

conversation.speaker: Rina

question.jepang: 山田(やまだ)さんなら安心(あんしん)ですね。営業(えいぎょう)の成績(せいせき)がいつもトップらしいですよ。

choices.arti: I'll become better than Yamada.

conversation.speaker: Rina

choices.jepang: スマホを見(み)ないと眠(ねむ)れないんですけど…。

choices.arti: If I don't look at my phone, I can't sleep...

conversation.explanation: The correct answer accepts advice and shows a specific plan using ~ようにする. Meanwhile, 'Can't sleep without phone' refuses the advice, and 'Nothing to do with it' denies without reason.

conversation.speaker: Dr. Sato

question.jepang: 軽(かる)い運動(うんどう)も始(はじ)めた方(ほう)がいいですよ。二週間後(にしゅうかんご)にまた来(き)てくださいね。
question.romaji: Karui undou mo hajimeta hou ga ii desu yo. Nishuukango ni mata kite kudasai ne.
question.arti: You should start light exercise too. Come back in two weeks.

choices.jepang: はい、毎朝(まいあさ)散歩(さんぽ)することにします。先生(せんせい)、ありがとうございました。
choices.romaji: Hai, maiasa sanpo suru koto ni shimasu. Sensei, arigatou gozaimashita.
choices.arti: Okay, I'll decide to walk every morning. Thank you, Doctor.

choices.jepang: 運動(うんどう)は嫌(きら)いです。
choices.romaji: Undou wa kirai desu.
choices.arti: I hate exercise.

choices.jepang: 運動(うんどう)する時間(じかん)がありません。
choices.romaji: Undou suru jikan ga arimasen.
choices.arti: No time for exercise.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer accepts advice and makes a concrete decision using ~ことにする. Meanwhile, 'No time' refuses advice, and 'I hate exercise' is negative and unconstructive.

conversation.speaker: Dr. Sato

question.jepang: あと、水分(すいぶん)もしっかり取(と)るようにしてくださいね。コーヒーばかり飲(の)んでいませんか？
question.romaji: Ato, suibun mo shikkari toru you ni shite kudasai ne. Koohii bakari nonde imasen ka?
question.arti: Also, make sure to drink enough water. You're not just drinking coffee, right?

choices.jepang: 水分(すいぶん)は十分(じゅうぶん)取(と)っています。
choices.romaji: Suibun wa juubun totte imasu.
choices.arti: I drink enough water.

choices.jepang: コーヒーは体(からだ)にいいと聞(き)きました。
choices.romaji: Koohii wa karada ni ii to kikimashita.
choices.arti: They say coffee is good for the body.

choices.jepang: 実(じつ)は、一日(いちにち)にコーヒーを五杯(ごはい)以上(いじょう)飲(の)んでしまっています。水(みず)はほとんど飲(の)んでいません。
choices.romaji: Jitsu wa, ichinichi ni koohii wo gohai ijou nonde shimatte imasu. Mizu wa hotondo nonde imasen.
choices.arti: Actually, I drink more than 5 cups of coffee a day. I hardly drink water.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer honestly explains bad habits using ~てしまう. Meanwhile, 'Coffee is good for the body' refuses advice, and 'I drink enough water' is not honest.

conversation.speaker: Dr. Sato

question.jepang: コーヒーの飲(の)みすぎは頭痛(ずつう)の原因(げんいん)になることがありますよ。一日(いちにち)二杯(にはい)までに減(へ)らした方(ほう)がいいですね。
question.romaji: Koohii no nomisgi wa zutsuu no gen'in ni naru koto ga arimasu yo. Ichinichi nihai made ni herashita hou ga ii desu ne.
question.arti: Too much coffee can cause headaches. You should cut down to two cups a day.

choices.jepang: コーヒーをやめるのは無理(むり)です。
choices.romaji: Koohii wo yameru no wa muri desu.
choices.arti: Quitting coffee is impossible.

choices.jepang: そうだったんですか。コーヒーのせいで頭(あたま)が痛(いた)くなっていたとは知(し)りませんでした。少(すこ)しずつ減(へ)らすようにします。
choices.romaji: Sou dattan desu ka. Koohii no sei de atama ga itaku natte ita to wa shirimasen deshita. Sukoshi zutsu herasu you ni shimasu.
choices.arti: Oh I see. I didn't know coffee could cause headaches. I'll try to reduce gradually.

choices.jepang: 二杯(にはい)じゃ足(た)りません。
choices.romaji: Nihai ja tarimasen.
choices.arti: Two cups isn't enough.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer humbly accepts new info and commits to change using ~ようにする. Meanwhile, 'Quitting is impossible' refuses advice, and 'Two cups isn't enough' disregards medical advice.

conversation.speaker: Dr. Sato

question.jepang: それから、ストレスもたまっているようですね。趣味(しゅみ)など、リラックスできることはありますか？
question.romaji: Sorekara, sutoresu mo tamatte iru you desu ne. Shumi nado, rirakkusu dekiru koto wa arimasu ka?
question.arti: Also, stress seems to be building up. Any hobbies or ways to relax?

choices.jepang: 前(まえ)は読書(どくしょ)が好(す)きだったんですが、忙(いそが)しくなってから本(ほん)を読(よ)む時間(じかん)がなくなってしまったんです。
choices.romaji: Mae wa dokusho ga suki dattan desu ga, isogashiku natte kara hon wo yomu jikan ga nakunatte shimattan desu.
choices.arti: I used to like reading, but since getting busy, I lost time for it.

choices.jepang: ゲームを朝(あさ)までやっています。
choices.romaji: Geemu wo asa made yatte imasu.
choices.arti: Playing games until morning.

choices.jepang: 趣味(しゅみ)なんてありません。
choices.romaji: Shumi nante arimasen.
choices.arti: I don't have hobbies.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer explains lifestyle changes using ~てしまう. Meanwhile, 'No hobbies' closes off solutions, and 'Games until morning' worsens the sleep problem.

conversation.speaker: Dr. Sato

question.jepang: リラックスする時間(じかん)を作(つく)ることも大切(たいせつ)ですよ。では、念(ねん)のために血液(けつえき)検査(けんさ)もしておきましょう。
question.romaji: Rirakkusu suru jikan wo tsukuru koto mo taisetsu desu yo. Dewa, nen no tame ni ketsueki kensa mo shite okimashou.
question.arti: Making relaxation time is important too. Just in case, let's do a blood test.

choices.jepang: そこまでしなくてもいいと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Soko made shinakute mo ii to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Not necessary to go that far.

choices.jepang: 血液(けつえき)検査(けんさ)は怖(こわ)いのでやめてください。
choices.romaji: Ketsueki kensa wa kowai node yamete kudasai.
choices.arti: I'm afraid of blood tests, please don't.

choices.jepang: はい、お願(ねが)いします。結果(けっか)はいつ出(で)るんですか？
choices.romaji: Hai, onegai shimasu. Kekka wa itsu derun desu ka?
choices.arti: Okay, please do. When will the results come?

conversation.explanation: The correct answer accepts the medical procedure and asks for important info. Meanwhile, 'Afraid of blood tests' refuses important examination, and 'Not necessary' ignores professional advice.

conversation.speaker: Dr. Sato

question.jepang: 三日後(みっかご)には出(で)るはずです。結果(けっか)が出(で)たら、また来(き)てくださいね。それまでに生活(せいかつ)改善(かいぜん)を始(はじ)めておいてください。
question.romaji: Mikkago ni wa deru hazu desu. Kekka ga detara, mata kite kudasai ne. Sore made ni seikatsu kaizen wo hajimete oite kudasai.
question.arti: Results should come in three days. When ready, come back. Until then, start improving your lifestyle.

choices.jepang: 分(わ)かりました。先生(せんせい)に言(い)われた通(とお)りに、食事(しょくじ)と睡眠(すいみん)を改善(かいぜん)するようにします。
choices.romaji: Wakarimashita. Sensei ni iwareta toori ni, shokuji to suimin wo kaizen suru you ni shimasu.
choices.arti: Okay. As the doctor said, I'll try to improve my eating and sleeping.

choices.jepang: 薬(くすり)を出(だ)してもらえませんか？
choices.romaji: Kusuri wo dashite moraemasen ka?
choices.arti: Can I get a prescription?

choices.jepang: 三日(みっか)も待(ま)てません。
choices.romaji: Mikka mo matemasen.
choices.arti: Can't wait three days.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer shows compliance and concrete plan using ~通りに and ~ようにする. Meanwhile, 'Can't wait' is impatient, and 'Prescription please' still seeks shortcuts.

conversation.speaker: Dr. Sato

question.jepang: はい、それが一番(いちばん)大事(だいじ)です。無理(むり)をしすぎないようにしてくださいね。お大事(だいじ)に。
question.romaji: Hai, sore ga ichiban daiji desu. Muri wo shisuginai you ni shite kudasai ne. Odaiji ni.
question.arti: Yes, that's most important. Don't push yourself too hard. Get well soon.

choices.jepang: できるかどうか分(わ)かりません。
choices.romaji: Dekiru ka dou ka wakarimasen.
choices.arti: Not sure if I can or not.

choices.jepang: 大丈夫(だいじょうぶ)です。心配(しんぱい)しないでください。
choices.romaji: Daijoubu desu. Shinpai shinaide kudasai.
choices.arti: It's okay. Don't worry.

choices.jepang: ありがとうございます。自分(じぶん)の体(からだ)を大切(たいせつ)にするようにします。先生(せんせい)、本当(ほんとう)にありがとうございました。
choices.romaji: Arigatou gozaimasu. Jibun no karada wo taisetsu ni suru you ni shimasu. Sensei, hontou ni arigatou gozaimashita.
choices.arti: Thank you. I'll try to take better care of myself. Doctor, thank you very much.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer politely closes and shows commitment using ~ようにする. Meanwhile, 'Don't worry' is odd since the patient should be the one being worried about, and 'Not sure' lacks motivation.

conversation.speaker: Yuki

question.jepang: 来週(らいしゅう)の連休(れんきゅう)に京都(きょうと)に行(い)こうと思(おも)っているんだけど、一緒(いっしょ)に行(い)かない？
question.romaji: Raishuu no renkyuu ni Kyouto ni ikou to omotte irun dakedo, issho ni ikanai?

choices.jepang: 京都(きょうと)？いいね！行(い)きたい！宿(やど)はもう予約(よやく)した？
choices.romaji: Kyouto? Ii ne! Ikitai! Yado wa mou yoyaku shita?

choices.romaji: Renkyuu wa shigoto ga aru.
choices.arti: I have work during the holiday.

choices.jepang: 京都(きょうと)は遠(とお)すぎる。

choices.arti: Kyoto is too far.

conversation.speaker: Yuki

question.romaji: Mada nanda. Ninki no hoteru wa yoyaku ga torinikui rashii kara, hayaku sagasanai to.

conversation.speaker: Yuki

question.romaji: Ii ne! Kyouto ni ittara, Arashiyama no chikurin ni zettai ikitain da.

choices.jepang: 竹林(ちくりん)はつまらないよ。

choices.arti: The bamboo grove is boring.

choices.jepang: 竹林(ちくりん)か！朝(あさ)早(はや)く行(い)けば、人(ひと)が少(すく)なくて写真(しゃしん)が撮(と)りやすいはずだよ。
choices.romaji: Chikurin ka! Asa hayaku ikeba, hito ga sukunakute shashin ga toriyasui hazu da yo.

choices.jepang: 写真(しゃしん)より動画(どうが)がいい。

Meanwhile, 'The bamboo grove is boring' rejects the friend's wish, and 'Videos are better than photos' changes the topic.

choices.jepang: 六時(ろくじ)に出(で)れば間(ま)に合(あ)うと思(おも)う。乗(の)り換(か)えが二回(にかい)あるみたいだよ。

choices.arti: If we leave at six, we should make it in time. It seems like we'd need to transfer twice.

choices.jepang: 昼(ひる)から行(い)こうよ。

conversation.speaker: Yuki

choices.romaji: Un! Hitsuyou na mono no risuto mo tsukutte oku yo. Zettai tanoshii tabi ni naru hazu da yo!

choices.jepang: いいね！着物(きもの)を着(き)て写真(しゃしん)を撮(と)ったら、いい思(おも)い出(で)になるはずだよ。予約(よやく)しておこう！

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'Awesome! If we wear kimono and take photos, it'll definitely be a great memory. Let's book in advance!' because it responds to the invitation enthusiastically and shows planning using ~たら, ~はずだ, and ~ておく. Meanwhile, 'Kimono makes it hard to move, let's not' rejects the idea, and 'It seems expensive, better not' also rejects without checking first.

conversation.speaker: Yuki

question.jepang: あ、お土産(みやげ)も買(か)わなきゃね。誰(だれ)に何(なに)を買(か)えばいいか迷(まよ)うなあ。

question.arti: Oh, we need to buy souvenirs too. I can't decide what to get for whom.

choices.romaji: Omiyage nante kawanakute ii yo.
choices.arti: No need to buy souvenirs.

choices.jepang: 京都(きょうと)なら、八(や)つ橋(はし)が有名(ゆうめい)だから、会社(かいしゃ)の人(ひと)にはそれを買(か)っておけばいいんじゃない？
choices.romaji: Kyouto nara, yatsuhashi ga yuumei dakara, kaisha no hito ni wa sore wo katte okeba iin ja nai?
choices.arti: For Kyoto, yatsuhashi is famous, right? How about getting those for your coworkers?

choices.jepang: 空港(くうこう)で買(か)えばいいよ。

conversation.speaker: Yuki

question.jepang: 八(や)つ橋(はし)、いいね！交通費(こうつうひ)はどうする？新幹線(しんかんせん)だと高(たか)いよね。
question.romaji: Yatsuhashi, ii ne! Koutsuuhi wa dou suru? Shinkansen da to takai yo ne.
question.arti: Yatsuhashi, nice! What about transportation costs? The shinkansen is expensive, right?

choices.jepang: 歩(ある)いて行(い)こう。
choices.romaji: Aruite ikou.
choices.arti: Let's just walk.

choices.jepang: お金(かね)のことは気(き)にしなくていいよ。
choices.romaji: Okane no koto wa ki ni shinakute ii yo.
choices.arti: Don't worry about money.

choices.jepang: 高速(こうそく)バスなら新幹線(しんかんせん)の半額(はんがく)ぐらいで行(い)けるはずだよ。時間(じかん)はかかるけど、節約(せつやく)するためにはいい方法(ほうほう)だと思(おも)う。
choices.romaji: Kousoku basu nara shinkansen no hangaku gurai de ikeru hazu da yo. Jikan wa kakaru kedo, setsuyaku suru tame ni wa ii houhou da to omou.
choices.arti: With a highway bus, it should cost about half the shinkansen fare. It takes longer, but it's a great way to save money.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer provides an affordable alternative using ~なら, ~はずだ, and ~ために. Meanwhile, 'Don't worry about money' isn't realistic, and 'Let's just walk' is impossible.

conversation.speaker: Yuki

question.romaji: Kousoku basu ka! Yakou basu nara asa tsuku kara, jikan mo muda ni naranai ne.

choices.romaji: Basu no naka de nereba daijoubu da yo.
choices.arti: Sleeping on the bus will be fine.

choices.jepang: うん、でも寝(ね)られるかどうか心配(しんぱい)だから、ネックピローを持(も)っていった方(ほう)がいいよ。疲(つか)れたまま観光(かんこう)するのは大変(たいへん)だからね。
choices.romaji: Un, demo nerareru ka dou ka shinpai dakara, nekkupiroo wo motte itta hou ga ii yo. Tsukareta mama kankou suru no wa taihen dakara ne.
choices.arti: Yeah, but I'm worried about not being able to sleep, so we should bring a neck pillow. Sightseeing while exhausted is tough.

choices.jepang: やっぱり新幹線(しんかんせん)にしよう。
choices.romaji: Yappari shinkansen ni shiyou.
choices.arti: Let's just take the shinkansen then.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer gives practical tips using ~た方がいい and explains the reasoning using ~たまま. Meanwhile, 'Sleeping on the bus will be fine' is too simplistic, and 'Let's just take the shinkansen' changes an already-made decision.

conversation.speaker: Yuki

question.jepang: なるほど、準備(じゅんび)をちゃんとしておかないとね。カメラも持(も)っていこう！
question.romaji: Naruhodo, junbi wo chanto shite okanai to ne. Kamera mo motte ikou!
question.arti: Right, we really need to prepare properly. Let's bring a camera too!

choices.arti: Just look at photos on social media.

choices.jepang: スマホで十分(じゅうぶん)だよ。

choices.jepang: いいね！せっかく京都(きょうと)に行(い)くんだから、きれいな写真(しゃしん)をたくさん撮(と)れるように練習(れんしゅう)しておくよ。
choices.romaji: Ii ne! Sekkaku Kyouto ni ikun dakara, kirei na shashin wo takusan toreru you ni renshuu shite oku yo.
choices.arti: Great! Since we rarely go to Kyoto, I'll practice beforehand so I can take lots of good photos.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer shows enthusiasm using せっかく~んだから and preparation using ~ように and ~ておく. Meanwhile, 'A phone is enough' lacks enthusiasm, and 'Just look at photos on social media' doesn't respond to the invitation.

conversation.speaker: Yuki

question.jepang: 楽(たの)しみだね！いい旅(たび)にしようね！
question.romaji: Tanoshimi da ne! Ii tabi ni shiyou ne!

choices.jepang: まあ、普通(ふつう)の旅行(りょこう)になるでしょ。
choices.romaji: Maa, futsuu no ryokou ni naru desho.
choices.arti: Yeah, it'll probably just be an ordinary trip.

choices.romaji: Tenki ga warukattara saiaku da ne.
choices.arti: If the weather is bad, that would be terrible.

choices.jepang: うん！二人(ふたり)で行(い)けば絶対(ぜったい)楽(たの)しいはずだよ。忘(わす)れられない思(おも)い出(で)を作(つく)ろう！
choices.romaji: Un! Futari de ikeba zettai tanoshii hazu da yo. Wasurerarenai omoide wo tsukurou!
choices.arti: Yes! Going together will definitely be fun. Let's make unforgettable memories!

conversation.explanation: The correct answer shows enthusiasm and confidence using ~ば, ~はずだ, and the potential passive form 忘れられない. Meanwhile, 'it'll just be an ordinary trip' lacks enthusiasm, and 'If the weather is bad, that would be terrible' focuses on negatives.

conversation.speaker: Ami

question.jepang: 最近(さいきん)、ルームメイトのことで悩(なや)んでいるんだ。ちょっと聞(き)いてくれない？
question.romaji: Saikin, ruumumeito no koto de nayande irun da. Chotto kiite kurenai?
question.arti: Lately, I've been stressed about my roommate. Can you listen for a moment?

choices.jepang: 自分(じぶん)で解決(かいけつ)しなよ。
choices.romaji: Jibun de kaiketsu shina yo.
choices.arti: Deal with it yourself.

choices.jepang: ごめん、今(いま)忙(いそが)しい。
choices.romaji: Gomen, ima isogashii.
choices.arti: Sorry, I'm busy right now.

choices.jepang: もちろん。どうしたの？
choices.romaji: Mochiron. Doushita no?
choices.arti: Of course. What's wrong?

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'Of course. What's wrong?' because it responds to the request to talk with empathy and encourages the friend to share. Meanwhile, 'Sorry, I'm busy right now' rejects a friend in need, and 'Deal with it yourself' shows no concern.

question.jepang: 彼女(かのじょ)、夜中(よなか)に音楽(おんがく)を聴(き)くのをやめようとしないの。何度(なんど)も頼(たの)んだのに。
question.romaji: Kanojo, yonaka ni ongaku wo kiku no wo yameyou to shinai no. Nando mo tanonda noni.
question.arti: She won't stop listening to music in the middle of the night. Even though I've asked her many times.

choices.jepang: それは大変(たいへん)だね。イヤホンを使(つか)ってもらうように頼(たの)んでみたら？
choices.romaji: Sore wa taihen da ne. Iyahon wo tsukatte morau you ni tanonde mitara?
choices.arti: That's tough. How about asking her to use earphones?

choices.jepang: 音楽(おんがく)ぐらい我慢(がまん)すればいいのに。
choices.romaji: Ongaku gurai gaman sureba ii noni.
choices.arti: It's just music, just put up with it.

choices.jepang: 引(ひ)っ越(こ)しなよ。
choices.romaji: Hikkoshi na yo.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'That's tough. How about asking her to use earphones?' because it shows empathy and offers a practical solution using ~てもらうように頼む. Meanwhile, 'It's just music, just put up with it' dismisses the problem, and 'Just move out' is too drastic without trying other solutions first.

conversation.speaker: Ami

question.jepang: それも言(い)ったんだけど、「耳(みみ)が痛(いた)くなる」って言(い)われたの。
question.romaji: Sore mo ittan dakedo, 'mimi ga itaku naru' tte iwareta no.
question.arti: I've already told her that, but she said 'it hurts her ears'.

choices.jepang: 嘘(うそ)に決(き)まってるよ。
choices.romaji: Uso ni kimatteru yo.
choices.arti: She's definitely lying.

choices.jepang: 仕方(しかた)がないね。諦(あきら)めよう。
choices.romaji: Shikata ga nai ne. Akirameyou.
choices.arti: What can you do. Just give up.

choices.jepang: 困(こま)ったね。せめて音量(おんりょう)を下(さ)げてもらえないかな。
choices.romaji: Komatta ne. Semete onryou wo sagete moraenai ka na.
choices.arti: That's tricky. At least, could you ask her to turn down the volume?

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'That's tricky. At least, could you ask her to turn down the volume?' because it acknowledges the difficulty and offers a compromise using ~てもらえないかな. Meanwhile, 'What can you do. Just give up' is too passive, and 'She's definitely lying' accuses without basis.

conversation.speaker: Ami

question.jepang: 彼女(かのじょ)と話(はな)し合(あ)おうとすると、いつも怒(おこ)り出(だ)すんだ。
question.romaji: Kanojo to hanashiaou to suru to, itsumo okori dasun da.

choices.arti: Even if she gets mad, just keep telling her.

choices.jepang: 管理人(かんりにん)に言(い)えば？

choices.jepang: 直接(ちょくせつ)が難(むずか)しいなら、手紙(てがみ)を書(か)いてみたら？気持(きも)ちが伝(つた)わりやすいかもしれないよ。

choices.arti: If talking directly is difficult, how about writing a letter? Your feelings might be conveyed more easily.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'If talking directly is difficult, how about writing a letter? Your feelings might be conveyed more easily' because it offers a creative alternative using ~なら and ~やすい. Meanwhile, 'Even if she gets mad, just keep telling her' could worsen the situation, and 'Tell the building manager?' may not be the right first step.

question.romaji: Tegami ka... yatte miyou ka na. Dou kakeba ii?

choices.jepang: LINEで送(おく)った方(ほう)が早(はや)いよ。
choices.romaji: LINE de okutta hou ga hayai yo.
choices.arti: Sending it via LINE is faster.

choices.jepang: 怒(おこ)らないように、優(やさ)しい言葉(ことば)で書(か)いた方(ほう)がいいよ。「お願(ねが)い」という形(かたち)にすれば、受(う)け入(い)れてもらいやすいはずだよ。

choices.arti: To avoid making her angry, write using gentle words. If you phrase it as a 'request', it should be easier to accept.

Meanwhile, 'Write down all your complaints' could make the roommate angry, and 'Sending it via LINE is faster' removes the personal touch of a letter.

conversation.speaker: Ami

choices.jepang: それだけじゃ足(た)りないよ。

choices.jepang: それはいい考(かんが)えだね。お互(たが)いが納得(なっとく)できるルールを話(はな)し合(あ)って決(き)めれば、トラブルが減(へ)るようになるはずだよ。
choices.romaji: Sore wa ii kangae da ne. Otagai ga nattoku dekiru ruuru wo hanashiatte kimereba, toraburu ga heru you ni naru hazu da yo.
choices.arti: That's a good idea. If you discuss and set rules that both of you can accept, the problems should decrease.

choices.jepang: ルールなんて守(まも)らないよ。

question.arti: During the discussion, I need to be careful not to get emotional. Because last time she ended up getting mad at me.

choices.jepang: 怒(おこ)られても気(き)にしなくていいよ。

choices.jepang: 「私(わたし)はこう感(かん)じている」という言(い)い方(かた)をすれば、相手(あいて)を責(せ)めているように聞(き)こえないはずだよ。
choices.romaji: 'Watashi wa kou kanjite iru' to iu iikata wo sureba, aite wo semete iru you ni kikoenai hazu da yo.
choices.arti: If you say 'I feel this way', it shouldn't sound like you're blaming the other person.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer provides a concrete communication technique using ~ば and ~はずだ. Meanwhile, 'Don't worry about being scolded' isn't helpful, and 'Just ask a friend to come along' could make the atmosphere awkward.

conversation.speaker: Ami

question.jepang: なるほど、そういう伝(つた)え方(かた)なら受(う)け入(い)れてもらいやすいかもね。
question.romaji: Naruhodo, sou iu tsutaekata nara ukeirete moraiyasui kamo ne.

choices.jepang: とにかく早(はや)く言(い)った方(ほう)がいいよ。
choices.romaji: Tonikaku hayaku itta hou ga ii yo.
choices.arti: Just say it quickly.

choices.jepang: 褒(ほ)める必要(ひつよう)はないよ。

conversation.explanation: The correct answer teaches a positive approach using ~ようにすれば and ~やすくなるはずだ. Meanwhile, 'No need to compliment her' lacks wisdom, and 'Just say it quickly' ignores the delivery method.

conversation.speaker: Ami

question.jepang: ありがとう。今度(こんど)の週末(しゅうまつ)に話(はな)し合(あ)うことにする。うまくいくように頑張(がんば)るね。

choices.jepang: まあ、無理(むり)しないでね。

choices.romaji: Ouen shite iru yo! Kitto ii houkou ni mukau hazu da yo. Mata nanika attara itsu demo soudan shite ne.
choices.arti: I'm rooting for you! Things will definitely get better. If anything else comes up, you can talk to me anytime.

choices.romaji: Gojikan mo? Sugoi ne. Demo, muri shisuginai you ni ne.
choices.arti: Five hours?! That's impressive. But don't push yourself too hard, okay?

choices.jepang: 五時間(ごじかん)じゃ足(た)りないよ。
choices.romaji: Gojikan ja tarinai yo.
choices.arti: Five hours isn't enough.

choices.jepang: 大学(だいがく)なんて行(い)かなくてもいい。
choices.romaji: Daigaku nante ikanakute mo ii.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'Five hours?! That's impressive. But don't push yourself too hard, okay?' because it appreciates the friend's effort while reminding them to stay healthy using ~すぎないように. Meanwhile, 'Five hours isn't enough' pressures the friend, and 'You don't need to go to college' rejects the friend's dream.

conversation.speaker: Kenji

conversation.speaker: Kenji

question.jepang: 実(じつ)は来月(らいげつ)から塾(じゅく)に通(かよ)うことになったんだ。親(おや)が申(もう)し込(こ)んでくれたんだよ。

choices.arti: If it hurts, just take some medicine.

choices.jepang: その通(とお)り！でも、筋肉痛(きんにくつう)が治(なお)らないうちに無理(むり)して走(はし)ると、怪我(けが)をしてしまうから、休(やす)む日(ひ)も作(つく)った方(ほう)がいいよ。

conversation.explanation: The correct answer gives healthy advice using ~ないうちに and ~てしまう. Meanwhile, 'Don't worry about it' ignores body signals, and 'Just take medicine' only treats symptoms.

conversation.speaker: Daichi

question.arti: Next month I'm moving. I decided to live near the office.

choices.jepang: 引(ひ)っ越(こ)しは大変(たいへん)だからやめた方(ほう)がいいよ。
choices.romaji: Hikkoshi wa taihen dakara yameta hou ga ii yo.
choices.arti: Moving is a hassle, better not.

choices.jepang: 今(いま)の家(いえ)の方(ほう)がいいんじゃない？
choices.romaji: Ima no ie no hou ga iin ja nai?

choices.jepang: そうなんだ！通勤(つうきん)時間(じかん)が短(みじか)くなれば、朝(あさ)もゆっくりできるようになるね。
choices.romaji: Sou nan da! Tsuukin jikan ga mijikaku nareba, asa mo yukkuri dekiru you ni naru ne.
choices.arti: Oh nice! If the commute is shorter, mornings will be more relaxed.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer responds positively seeing the benefit using ~ば and ~ようになる. Meanwhile, 'Moving is a hassle, don't bother' rejects the decision, and 'Your current place is better' doubts the friend's choice.

conversation.speaker: Megumi

question.jepang: うん。でも、荷物(にもつ)が多(おお)くて、一人(ひとり)では運(はこ)べないんだよね。
question.romaji: Un. Demo, nimotsu ga ookute, hitori de wa hakobenain da yo ne.
question.arti: Yeah. But I have a lot of stuff and can't carry it alone.

choices.jepang: 何回(なんかい)かに分(わ)けて運(はこ)べばいいよ。
choices.romaji: Nankai ka ni wakete hakobeba ii yo.
choices.arti: Just carry it in multiple trips.

choices.arti: Just throw away everything you don't need.

choices.jepang: 引(ひ)っ越(こ)し業者(ぎょうしゃ)に頼(たの)んだ方(ほう)がいいよ。重(おも)い物(もの)も運(はこ)んでもらえるし、楽(らく)だよ。
choices.romaji: Hikkoshi gyousha ni tanonda hou ga ii yo. Omoi mono mo hakonde moraeru shi, raku da yo.
choices.arti: Better to use a moving service. They can carry heavy items too, making it easier.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer gives practical advice using ~た方がいい and ~てもらえる. Meanwhile, 'Throw away everything' is too drastic, and 'Make multiple trips' is inefficient.

conversation.speaker: Megumi

question.jepang: そうだね。あと、引(ひ)っ越(こ)す前(まえ)に不用品(ふようひん)を整理(せいり)しないといけないんだけど、なかなか捨(す)てられなくて。
question.romaji: Sou da ne. Ato, hikkosu mae ni fuyouhin wo seiri shinai to ikenain dakedo, nakanaka suterarenakute.
question.arti: Good point. Before moving I need to sort out unused items, but it's hard to throw things away.

choices.arti: Don't think about it, just throw everything away.

choices.jepang: 捨(す)てるのがもったいないなら、フリマアプリで売(う)ってみたら？お金(かね)にもなるし、誰(だれ)かに使(つか)ってもらえるから。
choices.romaji: Suteru no ga mottainai nara, furima apuri de utte mitara? Okane ni mo naru shi, dareka ni tsukatte moraeru kara.
choices.arti: If it's hard to throw away, how about selling on a flea market app? You'll earn money and others can use them.

choices.romaji: Zenbu motte ikeba ii jan.

conversation.speaker: Megumi

question.jepang: それはいいアイデアだね。新(あたら)しい部屋(へや)はまだ何(なに)も揃(そろ)っていないから、家具(かぐ)も買(か)わなきゃ。
question.romaji: Sore wa ii aidea da ne. Atarashii heya wa mada nani mo sorotte inai kara, kagu mo kawanakya.

choices.romaji: IKEA nara yasukute oshare na kagu ga mitsukaru hazu da yo. Kumitate wa jibun de yaranakya ikenai kedo, setsumeisho doori ni yareba kantan da yo.
choices.arti: IKEA should have affordable and stylish furniture. You have to assemble it yourself, but following the manual is easy.

choices.jepang: 家具(かぐ)なんていらないよ。
choices.romaji: Kagu nante iranai yo.

choices.jepang: 高(たか)い家具(かぐ)を買(か)った方(ほう)がいいよ。
choices.romaji: Takai kagu wo katta hou ga ii yo.
choices.arti: Better to buy expensive furniture.

conversation.speaker: Megumi

choices.romaji: Sono hi wa yotei ga aru.
choices.arti: I have plans that day.

choices.jepang: 業者(ぎょうしゃ)に任(まか)せた方(ほう)がいいよ。
choices.romaji: Gyousha ni makaseta hou ga ii yo.
choices.arti: Just leave it to the movers.

choices.jepang: もちろん！手伝(てつだ)ってあげるよ。何時(なんじ)に行(い)けばいい？
choices.romaji: Mochiron! Tetsudatte ageru yo. Nanji ni ikeba ii?
choices.arti: Of course! I'll help. What time should I come?

conversation.speaker: Megumi

question.romaji: Ureshii! Asa kuji ni kite moraeru to tasukaru n dakedo. Orei ni gohan wo ogoru ne!
question.arti: Great! If you can come at 9 AM, that would be a huge help. As thanks, I'll treat you to a meal!

choices.romaji: Ryoukai! Atarashii seikatsu ga tanoshiku naru you ni, kirei ni katazukeyou! Hikkoshi tanoshimi da ne!
choices.arti: Okay! Let's organize neatly so the new life will be enjoyable! I'm excited about the move!

choices.jepang: 九時(くじ)は早(はや)すぎるよ。昼(ひる)からにして。
choices.romaji: Kuji wa hayasugiru yo. Hiru kara ni shite.

conversation.speaker: Megumi

conversation.speaker: Megumi

question.romaji: Saikin kamera wo katte, shashin wo tori hajimetan dakedo, nakanaka umaku torenain da.

choices.jepang: アドバイスなんてもらっても意味(いみ)がないよ。

choices.arti: Better not use social media. It's a waste of time.

choices.romaji: Sou sou. Jouzu na hito no shashin wo mireba miru hodo, jibun no sutairu ga wakatte kuru hazu da yo.

choices.jepang: 他(ほか)の人(ひと)の真似(まね)をしても意味(いみ)がないよ。
choices.romaji: Hoka no hito no mane wo shite mo imi ga nai yo.
choices.arti: Copying others is pointless.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer supports the decision using ~ば~ほど (the more~the more). Meanwhile, 'Copying others is pointless' misunderstands learning from references, and 'Social media is a waste of time' rejects the agreed idea.

choices.romaji: Ii yo! Hana wo toru nara, asa no hikari ga kirei dakara, gozenchuu ni itta hou ga ii yo.

choices.arti: You'll improve quickly. If you keep shooting while enjoying it, you'll naturally get better.

choices.jepang: 高(たか)いレンズを買(か)えば上手(じょうず)になるよ。

question.jepang: 週末(しゅうまつ)に公園(こうえん)で撮(と)った写真(しゃしん)、見(み)てくれない？自分(じぶん)ではうまく撮(と)れたかどうか分(わ)からなくて。
question.romaji: Shuumatsu ni kouen de totta shashin, mite kurenai? Jibun de wa umaku toreta ka dou ka wakaranakute.
question.arti: Want to see the photos from the park this weekend? I can't tell if they're good or not.

choices.jepang: もちろん！見(み)せて。…わあ、この花(はな)の写真(しゃしん)、構図(こうず)がきれいだね。光(ひかり)の使(つか)い方(かた)も上手(じょうず)になっているよ。
choices.romaji: Mochiron! Misete. ...Waa, kono hana no shashin, kouzu ga kirei da ne. Hikari no tsukaikata mo jouzu ni natte iru yo.
choices.arti: Of course! Let me see... Wow, this flower photo has great composition. Your use of light is improving too.

choices.jepang: まだまだだね。
choices.romaji: Mada mada da ne.
choices.arti: Still a long way to go.

choices.jepang: 自分(じぶん)で判断(はんだん)すればいいよ。
choices.romaji: Jibun de handan sureba ii yo.
choices.arti: Just judge it yourself.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer praises specific progress using ~なっている. Meanwhile, 'Judge yourself' isn't helpful, and 'Still far off' is crushing.

conversation.speaker: Shota

question.jepang: 本当(ほんとう)に？嬉(うれ)しいな！でも、人物(じんぶつ)を撮(と)るのはまだ苦手(にがて)なんだ。
question.romaji: Hontou ni? Ureshii na! Demo, jinbutsu wo toru no wa mada nigate nan da.
question.arti: Really? I'm happy! But I still can't photograph people well.

choices.jepang: プロのカメラマンに教(おし)えてもらうしかない。
choices.romaji: Puro no kameraman ni oshiete morau shika nai.
choices.arti: You need to learn from a professional photographer.

choices.jepang: 人物(じんぶつ)を撮(と)る時(とき)は、相手(あいて)をリラックスさせることが大事(だいじ)らしいよ。自然(しぜん)な表情(ひょうじょう)を撮(と)れるようになれば、写真(しゃしん)がもっと生(い)き生(い)きしてくるはずだよ。
choices.romaji: Jinbutsu wo toru toki wa, aite wo rirakkusu saseru koto ga daiji rashii yo. Shizen na hyoujou wo toreru you ni nareba, shashin ga motto iki iki shite kuru hazu da yo.
choices.arti: When photographing people, they say it's important to make the subject relax (causative). If you can capture natural expressions, photos will be more lively.

choices.jepang: 人物(じんぶつ)はやめて風景(ふうけい)だけ撮(と)ればいいよ。
choices.romaji: Jinbutsu wa yamete fuukei dake toreba ii yo.
choices.arti: Skip people, just photograph landscapes.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer gives specific tips using the causative form リラックスさせる and ~ようになれば~はずだ. Meanwhile, 'Just stick to landscapes' avoids the challenge, and 'Need a professional' makes it difficult.

conversation.speaker: Shota

question.jepang: なるほど。被写体(ひしゃたい)に話(はな)しかけながら撮(と)れば、自然(しぜん)な写真(しゃしん)が撮(と)れるかもしれないね。
question.romaji: Naruhodo. Hishatai ni hanashikakenagara toreba, shizen na shashin ga toreru kamo shirenai ne.
question.arti: I see. If I chat with the subject while taking photos, the results might look natural.

choices.jepang: そうそう！あと、写真(しゃしん)コンテストに出(だ)してみたらどう？目標(もくひょう)があった方(ほう)がもっと頑張(がんば)れるようになるはずだよ。
choices.romaji: Sou sou! Ato, shashin kontesuto ni dashite mitara dou? Mokuhyou ga atta hou ga motto ganbareru you ni naru hazu da yo.
choices.arti: Exactly! Also, how about entering a photo contest? Having a goal should boost your motivation.

choices.jepang: コンテストに出(だ)しても勝(か)てないよ。
choices.romaji: Kontesuto ni dashite mo katenai yo.
choices.arti: You won't win a contest.

choices.jepang: コンテストはまだ早(はや)いよ。
choices.romaji: Kontesuto wa mada hayai yo.
choices.arti: A contest is too early.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer suggests a goal using ~たらどう and ~はずだ. Meanwhile, 'Too early for a contest' holds back progress, and 'You won't win' is pessimistic.

conversation.speaker: Shota

question.jepang: コンテストか！やってみようかな。写真(しゃしん)を始(はじ)めてから毎日(まいにち)が楽(たの)しくなったよ。ありがとう！
question.romaji: Kontesuto ka! Yatte miyou ka na. Shashin wo hajimete kara mainichi ga tanoshiku natta yo. Arigatou!
question.arti: A contest! I'll give it a try. Since starting photography, every day has been fun. Thanks!

choices.jepang: 趣味(しゅみ)はすぐ飽(あ)きるから気(き)をつけてね。
choices.romaji: Shumi wa sugu akiru kara ki wo tsukete ne.
choices.arti: Hobbies get boring quickly, be careful.

choices.jepang: まあ、楽(たの)しめればいいよね。
choices.romaji: Maa, tanoshimereba ii yo ne.
choices.arti: Well, if you're having fun, that's good.

choices.jepang: 素敵(すてき)な趣味(しゅみ)が見(み)つかってよかったね。これからもお互(たが)いの作品(さくひん)を見(み)せ合(あ)って、一緒(いっしょ)に上達(じょうたつ)していこうよ！
choices.romaji: Suteki na shumi ga mitsukatte yokatta ne. Kore kara mo otagai no sakuhin wo miseatte, issho ni joutatsu shite ikou yo!
choices.arti: Great that you found a nice hobby. Let's keep showing each other our work and grow together!

conversation.explanation: The correct answer responds positively and suggests growing together using ~て+いこう. Meanwhile, 'Hobbies get boring quickly' is pessimistic, and 'If you're having fun, that's good' is half-hearted.

conversation.speaker: Kana

question.jepang: 久(ひさ)しぶりに高校(こうこう)の友達(ともだち)に会(あ)ったんだけど、すごく変(か)わっていて驚(おどろ)いたよ。
question.romaji: Hisashiburi ni koukou no tomodachi ni attan dakedo, sugoku kawatte ite odoroita yo.
question.arti: I met a high school friend after a long time, and they've changed so much -- I was surprised.

choices.jepang: へえ、どう変(か)わったの？
choices.romaji: Hee, dou kawatta no?
choices.arti: Wow, how did they change?

choices.jepang: そうなんだ。別(べつ)に興味(きょうみ)ないけど。
choices.romaji: Sou nan da. Betsu ni kyoumi nai kedo.
choices.arti: Oh really. Not interested though.

choices.jepang: 人(ひと)は変(か)わるものだよ。
choices.romaji: Hito wa kawaru mono da yo.
choices.arti: People do change.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'Wow, how did they change?' because it shows interest and encourages the friend to continue the story. Meanwhile, 'People do change' is too philosophical and closes the conversation, and 'Not interested' shows no concern.

conversation.speaker: Kana

question.jepang: 前(まえ)はおとなしい子(こ)だったのに、今(いま)は海外(かいがい)で一人(ひとり)で暮(く)らしているんだって。自信(じしん)に満(み)ちていたよ。
question.romaji: Mae wa otonashii ko datta noni, ima wa kaigai de hitori de kurashite irun datte. Jishin ni michite ita yo.
question.arti: Used to be a quiet kid, but now apparently living alone abroad. Full of confidence.

choices.jepang: 一人(ひとり)で海外(かいがい)なんて寂(さび)しそう。
choices.romaji: Hitori de kaigai nante sabishisou.
choices.arti: Living alone abroad seems lonely.

choices.jepang: すごいね。海外(かいがい)で暮(く)らすのは大変(たいへん)なはずだけど、それで成長(せいちょう)したんだね。
choices.romaji: Sugoi ne. Kaigai de kurasu no wa taihen na hazu dakedo, sore de seichou shitan da ne.
choices.arti: That's impressive. Living abroad must be tough, but that's how they grew, right?

choices.jepang: それは危(あぶ)ないよ。
choices.romaji: Sore wa abunai yo.
choices.arti: That's dangerous.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer appreciates the friend's change with empathy using ~はずだ. Meanwhile, 'Living alone abroad seems lonely' focuses on negatives, and 'That's dangerous' is overly worried without context.

conversation.speaker: Kana

question.jepang: うん。彼女(かのじょ)を見(み)て、私(わたし)も何(なに)か新(あたら)しいことに挑戦(ちょうせん)しようかなと思(おも)い始(はじ)めたんだ。
question.romaji: Un. Kanojo wo mite, watashi mo nanika atarashii koto ni chousen shiyou ka na to omoi hajimetan da.
question.arti: Yeah. Seeing them, I've also started thinking about trying new challenges.

choices.jepang: いいね！何(なに)かやりたいことがあるの？
choices.romaji: Ii ne! Nanika yaritai koto ga aru no?
choices.arti: Great! Is there something specific you want to do?

choices.jepang: 人(ひと)と比(くら)べても仕方(しかた)がないよ。
choices.romaji: Hito to kurabete mo shikata ga nai yo.
choices.arti: Comparing yourself to others is pointless.

choices.jepang: 無理(むり)しない方(ほう)がいいよ。
choices.romaji: Muri shinai hou ga ii yo.
choices.arti: Better not push yourself.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer supports and shows interest in the friend's plans. Meanwhile, 'Better not push yourself' hinders motivation, and 'Comparing yourself to others is pointless' misreads the situation since Kana is inspired, not jealous.

conversation.speaker: Kana

question.jepang: 実(じつ)は、前(まえ)から留学(りゅうがく)に興味(きょうみ)があったんだけど、勇気(ゆうき)が出(で)なくて。彼女(かのじょ)に背中(せなか)を押(お)された気(き)がする。
question.romaji: Jitsu wa, mae kara ryuugaku ni kyoumi ga attan dakedo, yuuki ga denakute. Kanojo ni senaka wo osareta ki ga suru.
question.arti: Actually, I've always been interested in studying abroad, but was too scared. It feels like they pushed my back.

choices.jepang: 留学(りゅうがく)はお金(かね)がかかるよ。
choices.romaji: Ryuugaku wa okane ga kakaru yo.
choices.arti: Studying abroad is expensive.

choices.jepang: 留学(りゅうがく)か！行(い)くなら、今(いま)のうちに語学力(ごがくりょく)を上(あ)げておいた方(ほう)がいいよ。準備(じゅんび)を始(はじ)めるなら早(はや)い方(ほう)がいいはずだよ。
choices.romaji: Ryuugaku ka! Iku nara, ima no uchi ni gogakuryoku wo agete oita hou ga ii yo. Junbi wo hajimeru nara hayai hou ga ii hazu da yo.
choices.arti: Study abroad! If you want to go, better start improving your language skills now. The sooner you prepare, the better.

choices.jepang: 日本(にほん)にいた方(ほう)が安全(あんぜん)だよ。
choices.romaji: Nihon ni ita hou ga anzen da yo.
choices.arti: It's safer to stay in Japan.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer gives concrete advice using ~なら, ~ておいた方がいい, and ~はずだ. Meanwhile, 'Studying abroad is expensive' focuses on barriers, and 'Japan is safer' is discouraging.

conversation.speaker: Kana

question.jepang: そうだね。まずは英語(えいご)の勉強(べんきょう)を始(はじ)めることにする。TOEFLも受(う)けなきゃいけないらしいし。
question.romaji: Sou da ne. Mazu wa eigo no benkyou wo hajimeru koto ni suru. TOEFL mo ukenakya ikenai rashii shi.
question.arti: Good point. First, I'll decide to start studying English. They say I need to take the TOEFL too.

choices.jepang: 独学(どくがく)でやった方(ほう)がいいよ。
choices.romaji: Dokugaku de yatta hou ga ii yo.
choices.arti: Better to study on your own.

choices.jepang: TOEFLは難(むずか)しすぎるよ。
choices.romaji: TOEFL wa muzukashisugiru yo.
choices.arti: TOEFL is way too hard.

choices.jepang: 応援(おうえん)するよ！一緒(いっしょ)に英語(えいご)のカフェに行(い)ってみない？ネイティブと話(はな)せば、リスニング力(りょく)が上(あ)がるようになるはずだよ。
choices.romaji: Ouen suru yo! Issho ni eigo no kafe ni itte minai? Neitibu to hanaseba, risuninguryoku ga agaru you ni naru hazu da yo.
choices.arti: I support you! Want to try going to an English cafe together? Chatting with native speakers should improve your listening.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer offers concrete help using ~ば and ~ようになるはずだ. Meanwhile, 'TOEFL is too hard' crushes motivation, and 'Better study alone' refuses to help.

conversation.speaker: Kana

question.jepang: ありがとう！あの子(こ)みたいに成長(せいちょう)できるように、私(わたし)も頑張(がんば)る！
question.romaji: Arigatou! Ano ko mitai ni seichou dekiru you ni, watashi mo ganbaru!
question.arti: Thanks! I want to grow like them too. I'll do my best!

choices.jepang: まあ、頑張(がんば)れば何(なん)とかなるでしょ。
choices.romaji: Maa, ganbareba nantoka naru desho.
choices.arti: Yeah, if you work hard, you'll be fine.

choices.jepang: カナならきっと大丈夫(だいじょうぶ)だよ。夢(ゆめ)に向(む)かって一歩(いっぽ)ずつ進(すす)んでいこう！応援(おうえん)しているからね。
choices.romaji: Kana nara kitto daijoubu da yo. Yume ni mukatte ippo zutsu susunde ikou! Ouen shite iru kara ne.
choices.arti: If it's you, Kana, you'll be fine. Let's move step by step toward your dream! I'm supporting you.

choices.jepang: あまり期待(きたい)しない方(ほう)がいいよ。
choices.romaji: Amari kitai shinai hou ga ii yo.
choices.arti: Better not get your hopes up too much.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer shows confidence using ~なら and gives motivation. Meanwhile, 'Better not get your hopes up' is pessimistic, and 'Yeah, if you try hard' feels half-hearted.

conversation.speaker: Kana

question.jepang: 実(じつ)は、もう留学(りゅうがく)のプログラムを調(しら)べ始(はじ)めたんだ。カナダかオーストラリアに行(い)きたいなと思(おも)って。
question.romaji: Jitsu wa, mou ryuugaku no puroguramu wo shirabe hajimetan da. Kanada ka Oosutoraria ni ikitai na to omotte.
question.arti: Actually, I've started looking into study abroad programs. I'm thinking Canada or Australia.

choices.jepang: カナダは寒(さむ)すぎるよ。
choices.romaji: Kanada wa samusugiru yo.
choices.arti: Canada is way too cold.

choices.jepang: 両方(りょうほう)ともいい国(くに)だね。カナダは自然(しぜん)が豊(ゆた)かだし、オーストラリアは気候(きこう)がいいらしいよ。自分(じぶん)に合(あ)った環境(かんきょう)を選(えら)べばいいと思(おも)うよ。
choices.romaji: Ryouhou tomo ii kuni da ne. Kanada wa shizen ga yutaka da shi, Oosutoraria wa kikou ga ii rashii yo. Jibun ni atta kankyou wo erabeba ii to omou yo.
choices.arti: Both are great countries. Canada has rich nature, and Australia apparently has nice weather. Just pick the environment that suits you best.

choices.jepang: どっちでもいいんじゃない？
choices.romaji: Docchi demo iin ja nai?
choices.arti: Either one is fine, right?

conversation.explanation: The correct answer compares objectively using ~らしい and ~ば. Meanwhile, 'Too cold' only focuses on negatives, and 'Either one' isn't helpful.

conversation.speaker: Kana

question.jepang: 費用(ひよう)のことも考(かんが)えなきゃいけないんだよね。奨学金(しょうがくきん)がもらえるかどうか調(しら)べているところなんだ。
question.romaji: Hiyou no koto mo kangaenakya ikenain da yo ne. Shougakukin ga moraeru ka dou ka shirabete iru tokoro nan da.
question.arti: I also need to think about costs. I'm looking into whether I can get a scholarship.

choices.jepang: 奨学金(しょうがくきん)がもらえれば費用(ひよう)の負担(ふたん)が減(へ)るよね。申請(しんせい)の締(し)め切(き)りに遅(おく)れないように、早(はや)めに準備(じゅんび)しておいた方(ほう)がいいよ。
choices.romaji: Shougakukin ga moraereba hiyou no futan ga heru yo ne. Shinsei no shimekiri ni okurenai you ni, hayame ni junbi shite oita hou ga ii yo.
choices.arti: If you get a scholarship, the financial burden decreases. To avoid missing the application deadline, better prepare early.

choices.jepang: 親(おや)に出(だ)してもらえばいいじゃん。
choices.romaji: Oya ni dashite moraeba ii jan.
choices.arti: Just ask your parents to pay for it.

choices.jepang: 奨学金(しょうがくきん)は競争(きょうそう)が激(はげ)しいから無理(むり)だよ。
choices.romaji: Shougakukin wa kyousou ga hageshii kara muri da yo.
choices.arti: Scholarships are too competitive, it's impossible.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer gives practical advice using ~ば and ~ないように~ておいた方がいい. Meanwhile, 'Impossible' is pessimistic, and 'Ask your parents' is insensitive.

conversation.speaker: Kana

question.jepang: 英語(えいご)カフェにも通(かよ)い始(はじ)めたよ。最初(さいしょ)は緊張(きんちょう)したけど、話(はな)しているうちに楽(たの)しくなってきた。
question.romaji: Eigo kafe ni mo kayoi hajimeta yo. Saisho wa kinchou shita kedo, hanashite iru uchi ni tanoshiku natte kita.
question.arti: I've also been going to the English cafe regularly. I was nervous at first, but chatting has become more fun.

choices.jepang: お金(かね)がもったいないよ。
choices.romaji: Okane ga mottainai yo.
choices.arti: What a waste of money.

choices.jepang: カフェだけじゃ足(た)りないよ。
choices.romaji: Kafe dake ja tarinai yo.
choices.arti: Just a cafe isn't enough.

choices.jepang: すごい行動力(こうどうりょく)だね！続(つづ)けていれば、留学(りゅうがく)する前(まえ)にかなり話(はな)せるようになるはずだよ。
choices.romaji: Sugoi koudouryoku da ne! Tsuzukete ireba, ryuugaku suru mae ni kanari hanaseru you ni naru hazu da yo.
choices.arti: That's amazing initiative! If you keep it up, before you leave for study abroad, you should already be able to speak fairly well.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer appreciates the action and motivates using ~ていれば and ~ようになるはずだ. Meanwhile, 'Just a cafe isn't enough' is dismissive, and 'What a waste of money' is unsupportive.

conversation.speaker: Kana

question.jepang: ありがとう！夢(ゆめ)に向(む)かって頑張(がんば)るよ。いつか海外(かいがい)から手紙(てがみ)を送(おく)るね！
question.romaji: Arigatou! Yume ni mukatte ganbaru yo. Itsuka kaigai kara tegami wo okuru ne!
question.arti: Thanks! I'll work hard toward my dream. Someday I'll send you a letter from abroad!

choices.jepang: 寂(さび)しくなるなあ。
choices.romaji: Sabishiku naru naa.
choices.arti: I'll be lonely.

choices.jepang: 楽(たの)しみにしているよ！カナなら絶対(ぜったい)夢(ゆめ)を叶(かな)えられるはずだよ。離(はな)れていても、ずっと応援(おうえん)しているからね！
choices.romaji: Tanoshimi ni shite iru yo! Kana nara zettai yume wo kanaerareru hazu da yo. Hanarete ite mo, zutto ouen shite iru kara ne!
choices.arti: I'll be waiting! If it's you, Kana, you can definitely make your dream come true. Even far away, I'll always be supporting you!

choices.jepang: 手紙(てがみ)よりLINEでいいよ。
choices.romaji: Tegami yori LINE de ii yo.
choices.arti: Rather than a letter, just use LINE.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer shows total support using ~なら, ~はずだ, and ~ても~ている. Meanwhile, 'Just use LINE' removes the sentiment, and 'I'll be lonely' could make the friend feel guilty.

conversation.speaker: Mai

question.jepang: 猫(ねこ)を飼(か)いたいなあと思(おも)っているんだけど、アパートだから飼(か)えるかどうか分(わ)からなくて。
question.romaji: Neko wo kaitai naa to omotte irun dakedo, apaato dakara kaeru ka dou ka wakaranakute.
question.arti: I want to keep a cat, but since I live in an apartment, I don't know if I can.

choices.jepang: まず大家(おおや)さんに確認(かくにん)してみたら？ペット可(か)の物件(ぶっけん)なら飼(か)えるはずだよ。
choices.romaji: Mazu ooya-san ni kakunin shite mitara? Petto ka no bukken nara kaeru hazu da yo.
choices.arti: Try asking the landlord first? If pets are allowed, you should be fine.

choices.jepang: 猫(ねこ)より犬(いぬ)の方(ほう)がいいよ。
choices.romaji: Neko yori inu no hou ga ii yo.
choices.arti: Dogs are better than cats.

choices.jepang: アパートじゃ無理(むり)だよ。
choices.romaji: Apaato ja muri da yo.
choices.arti: You can't in an apartment.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer gives the right first step using ~てみたら, ~なら, and ~はずだ. Meanwhile, 'You can't in an apartment' may not be true, and 'Dogs are better than cats' doesn't answer the question.

conversation.speaker: Mai

question.jepang: 確認(かくにん)したら、猫(ねこ)は飼(か)ってもいいって言(い)われたの！でも、初(はじ)めてだから不安(ふあん)で。
question.romaji: Kakunin shitara, neko wa katte mo ii tte iwareta no! Demo, hajimete dakara fuan de.
question.arti: I asked and they said cats are allowed! But since it's my first time, I'm nervous.

choices.jepang: 私(わたし)は三年(さんねん)ぐらい猫(ねこ)を飼(か)っているから、何(なに)でも聞(き)いてね。飼(か)い始(はじ)めたら分(わ)からないことがたくさん出(で)てくるはずだから。
choices.romaji: Watashi wa sannen gurai neko wo katte iru kara, nan demo kiite ne. Kai hajimetara wakaranai koto ga takusan dete kuru hazu dakara.
choices.arti: I've been keeping a cat for about three years, so ask me anything. Once you start keeping one, there'll be lots you don't understand.

choices.jepang: ネットで調(しら)べれば大丈夫(だいじょうぶ)だよ。
choices.romaji: Netto de shirabeba daijoubu da yo.
choices.arti: Just look it up online and you'll be fine.

choices.jepang: 猫(ねこ)は手(て)がかかるからやめた方(ほう)がいいよ。
choices.romaji: Neko wa te ga kakaru kara yameta hou ga ii yo.
choices.arti: Cats are a hassle, better not.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer offers experience and support using ~たら and ~はずだ. Meanwhile, 'Cats are a hassle, better not' crushes motivation, and 'Just look it up online' is less personal.

conversation.speaker: Mai

choices.romaji: Ima no shigoto ni shuuchuu shita hou ga ii yo.
choices.arti: Better to focus on your current job.

choices.jepang: いい考(かんが)えだね。今(いま)の仕事(しごと)をしながらスキルアップすれば、いつでも転職(てんしょく)できる状態(じょうたい)を作(つく)れるわけだからね。
choices.romaji: Ii kangae da ne. Ima no shigoto wo shinagara sukiru appu sureba, itsu demo tenshoku dekiru joutai wo tsukureru wake dakara ne.
choices.arti: Good idea. If you improve skills while working, you'll be ready to change jobs anytime.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer supports the strategy using ~ながら and ~わけだから. Meanwhile, 'Doing both is impossible' is pessimistic, and 'Focus on current work' hinders growth.

conversation.speaker: Ryota

question.jepang: オンラインの講座(こうざ)を受(う)け始(はじ)めたんだけど、思(おも)ったより難(むずか)しいんだ。独学(どくがく)で本当(ほんとう)にできるようになるのかな。
question.romaji: Onrain no kouza wo uke hajimetan dakedo, omotta yori muzukashiin da. Dokugaku de hontou ni dekiru you ni naru no ka na.
question.arti: I started an online course, but it's harder than I imagined. Can I really get good by studying alone?

choices.jepang: 最初(さいしょ)は難(むずか)しく感(かん)じるのは当然(とうぜん)だよ。分(わ)からないところはコミュニティで質問(しつもん)すれば、教(おし)えてもらえるはずだから、一人(ひとり)で悩(なや)まないようにね。
choices.romaji: Saisho wa muzukashiku kanjiru no wa touzen da yo. Wakaranai tokoro wa komyunitii de shitsumon sureba, oshiete moraeru hazu dakara, hitori de nayamanai you ni ne.
choices.arti: Feeling it's hard at first is normal. If you ask questions in communities, you can get help, so don't struggle alone.

choices.jepang: 諦(あきら)めて学校(がっこう)に行(い)った方(ほう)がいいよ。
choices.romaji: Akiramete gakkou ni itta hou ga ii yo.
choices.arti: Just give up and go to school instead.

choices.jepang: やっぱり独学(どくがく)は無理(むり)だよ。
choices.romaji: Yappari dokugaku wa muri da yo.
choices.arti: Self-study really is impossible after all.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer reassures and gives a solution using ~ば~てもらえるはずだ and ~ないように. Meanwhile, 'It really is impossible' is crushing, and 'Give up and go to school' is too drastic.

conversation.speaker: Ryota

question.jepang: そうだね。プログラミングのコミュニティに参加(さんか)してみるよ。同(おな)じ目標(もくひょう)の人(ひと)がいれば心強(こころづよ)いしね。
question.romaji: Sou da ne. Puroguramingu no komyunitii ni sanka shite miru yo. Onaji mokuhyou no hito ga ireba kokorozuyoi shi ne.
question.arti: Good point. I'll try joining a programming community. Having people with the same goals should boost motivation.

choices.jepang: 一人(ひとり)でやった方(ほう)が集中(しゅうちゅう)できるよ。
choices.romaji: Hitori de yatta hou ga shuuchuu dekiru yo.
choices.arti: You can focus better alone.

choices.jepang: コミュニティは時間(じかん)の無駄(むだ)だよ。
choices.romaji: Komyunitii wa jikan no muda da yo.
choices.arti: Communities are a waste of time.

choices.jepang: うん、仲間(なかま)がいると続(つづ)けやすくなるはずだよ。お互(たが)い教(おし)え合(あ)えれば、もっと早(はや)く成長(せいちょう)できるしね。
choices.romaji: Un, nakama ga iru to tsuzukeyasuku naru hazu da yo. Otagai oshieaereba, motto hayaku seichou dekiru shi ne.
choices.arti: Yes, having friends makes it easier to continue. Teaching each other helps you grow faster too.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer supports the decision and explains benefits using ~やすくなるはずだ and ~ば. Meanwhile, 'Waste of time' is dismissive, and 'Studying alone is more focused' ignores community benefits.

conversation.speaker: Ryota

question.jepang: 最近(さいきん)、仕事(しごと)へのモチベーションも上(あ)がってきたんだ。新(あたら)しいプロジェクトは面白(おもしろ)いし、勉強(べんきょう)も充実(じゅうじつ)している。
question.romaji: Saikin, shigoto e no mochibeeshon mo agatte kitan da. Atarashii purojekuto wa omoshiroi shi, benkyou mo juujitsu shite iru.
question.arti: Lately my work motivation has gone up too. The new project is fun and studying is fulfilling.

choices.jepang: 転職(てんしょく)の話(はなし)はもうやめたの？
choices.romaji: Tenshoku no hanashi wa mou yameta no?
choices.arti: Already stopped thinking about changing jobs?

choices.jepang: 相談(そうだん)して本当(ほんとう)によかったね。行動(こうどう)を起(お)こしたからこそ、状況(じょうきょう)が変(か)わったわけだよ。
choices.romaji: Soudan shite hontou ni yokatta ne. Koudou wo okoshita kara koso, joukyou ga kawatta wake da yo.
choices.arti: I'm glad you consulted them. It's precisely because you took action that things changed.

choices.jepang: でも、また元(もと)に戻(もど)るかもしれないよ。
choices.romaji: Demo, mata moto ni modoru kamo shirenai yo.
choices.arti: But it might go back to how it was before.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer appreciates the courage using ~からこそ and ~わけだ. Meanwhile, 'Might go back to how it was' is pessimistic, and 'Already stopped thinking about changing jobs?' ignores the positive growth.

conversation.speaker: Ryota

question.jepang: ありがとう。あの時(とき)相談(そうだん)に乗(の)ってくれなかったら、ずっと悩(なや)んだままだったと思(おも)う。本当(ほんとう)に感謝(かんしゃ)しているよ。
question.romaji: Arigatou. Ano toki soudan ni notte kurenakattara, zutto nayanda mama datta to omou. Hontou ni kansha shite iru yo.
question.arti: Thanks. If you hadn't listened to me back then, I'd still be feeling lost. I'm truly grateful.

choices.jepang: 別(べつ)に大(たい)したことしていないよ。
choices.romaji: Betsu ni taishita koto shite inai yo.
choices.arti: I didn't really do anything.

choices.jepang: まあ、友達(ともだち)だからね。
choices.romaji: Maa, tomodachi dakara ne.
choices.arti: Well, that's what friends are for.

choices.jepang: そう言(い)ってもらえて嬉(うれ)しいよ。これからも何(なに)かあったら遠慮(えんりょ)なく言(い)ってね。リョウタの成長(せいちょう)を見(み)られて、私(わたし)も嬉(うれ)しいんだから。
choices.romaji: Sou itte moraete ureshii yo. Kore kara mo nanika attara enryo naku itte ne. Ryouta no seichou wo mirarete, watashi mo ureshiin dakara.
choices.arti: I'm glad to hear that. Going forward, if anything comes up, don't hesitate to talk to me. Seeing Ryota grow makes me happy too.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer shows happiness in seeing the friend's growth using ~てもらえて and the potential form 見られて. Meanwhile, 'I didn't do anything' understates the support, and 'That's what friends are for' is less sincere.
